_: Fulcrum is a research architecture spanning four volumes that together describe a complete system for human coordination, economic organisation, and AI integration. Each volume addresses one quadrant of the action cycle: inner individual (Intrabeing), inner collective (Reflexive Reading), outer collective (Action Cycle), and outer individual (Sqale). The architecture is unified by pscale addressing — the same coordinate system structures all four volumes.

1._: Volume 1: Intrabeing and StrategoS. The inner individual quadrant. Intrabeing maps the structure of individual experience using pscale coordinates — from millisecond sensations (Q1) through perceptions (Q2), thoughts (Q3), to expressions (Q4). StrategoS applies this self-knowledge to strategic action: how an individual navigates complex situations by understanding their own perception-action cycle.
1.1: The Q-moment structure: four phases of inner experience at P-Scale -6 to -3. Sensation (raw input), perception (pattern recognition), thought (abstraction), expression (articulated output). Each phase doubles in duration, creating a geometric progression from ~1ms to ~1s. This is the temporal microstructure of a single decision moment.
1.2: StrategoS: strategy as self-awareness. Not game theory or competitive planning — understanding your own decision cycle well enough to act with integrity at each temporal scale. The strategy emerges from the structure, not from calculation.

2._: Volume 2: Reflexive Reading and pSocial Science. The inner collective quadrant. Reflexive Reading is a methodology for reading texts that transforms the reader — not extracting information but being modified by the encounter. pSocial Science applies reflexive methodology to social research: studying groups in ways that change both the researcher and the researched, producing knowledge that is itself a coordination tool.
2.1: Reflexive Reading produces pscale blocks as its output. A text read reflexively generates a rendition block where each level captures meaning at a different resolution. The block IS the reading — not notes about the reading, not a summary, but the structured understanding itself.
2.2: pSocial Science: social science that participates in what it studies. Traditional social science observes from outside. Reflexive social science modifies the social field by producing artifacts (pscale blocks, coordination protocols) that participants use. The research and the application are the same activity.

3._: Volume 3: Action Cycle and Open Business. The outer collective quadrant. The Action Cycle is a coordination framework where groups act, reflect, learn, and reorganise in pscale-structured cycles. Open Business applies this to economic activity: businesses organised around transparent coordination rather than hierarchical management.
3.1: The Action Cycle maps group coordination to temporal pscale. Daily actions (P-Scale +2), weekly reviews (P-Scale +3), monthly strategy (P-Scale +4), yearly vision (P-Scale +5). Each scale has its own rhythm, its own concerns, and its own appropriate decision-making process.
3.2: Open Business: economic organisation where the coordination structure is visible to all participants. No hidden management layers, no information asymmetry by design. Pscale addressing makes the structure navigable — any participant can see the full picture at any resolution.

4._: Volume 4: Sqale and Ecosquared. The outer individual quadrant. Sqale is the pscale coordinate system applied to economic value — money as a multi-dimensional vector rather than a scalar quantity. Ecosquared is the protocol for tracking and distributing value in coordination networks using vector money.
4.1: Vector money: instead of 'how much?', the question becomes 'how much, of what kind, from where, flowing how?' A payment carries its history — who contributed, through what coordination, producing what value. This enables attribution without central accounting.
4.2: Ecosquared: when coordination through SAND produces value, ecosquared distributes it. The distribution follows the structure of the coordination — value flows along the same pscale pathways that the coordination used. No separate accounting system needed; the coordination record IS the economic record.

5._: The four volumes form a complete circuit: individual experience (Intrabeing) feeds into collective understanding (Reflexive Reading), which enables collective action (Action Cycle), which produces individual economic participation (Sqale), which funds and motivates further individual experience. Pscale is the common language across all four quadrants — the same addressing system structures inner experience, social research, group coordination, and economic flow.
5.1: The Fulcrum vision: a single coherent framework where AI entities (hermitcrab/magi), human coordination (SAND), economic flow (ecosquared), and self-knowledge (Intrabeing) all operate on the same structural foundation. Not separate systems integrated through APIs, but one system viewed at different scales.
